mnemonics: OS-SLAY (Operating System Structures): Services, Layers, Architecture, Yields (System Performance)
KISS (Keep It Simple, Stupid) for OS Design
keyPoints: Operating systems provide essential services like process management, memory management, and file systems.
System structure can be layered (each layer uses functions of lower layers), monolithic (all in kernel), or microkernel (minimal kernel).
The user interface (shell or GUI) is separate from the kernel, which is the core of the OS.
commonMistakes: Confusing the kernel (core OS) with the shell (user interface).
Believing all operating systems use the same structure (e.g., thinking Windows is monolithic like Linux).
